title: Designing the Forward Pass
concept: Routing input vectors through linear and activation layers to output raw probability scores (logits).

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of initializing weights in a neural network?

options.text: It makes the network run faster.
It helps start the training process stably.
It prevents overfitting from the beginning.

exercises.explain: Initializing weights correctly is vital as it ensures the training process begins in a stable manner, helping the model learn effectively.

cards.title: What is the Forward Pass?
cards.body: The forward pass is where the input data moves through different layers of the model, leading to an output.

cards.title: Forward Pass Definition
cards.body: In a forward pass, input vectors are processed through linear layers and activation functions to produce output scores.

cards.title: Forward Pass as a Journey
cards.body: Think of the forward pass like a road trip: the input is the departure point, and the output is your destination, with each layer being a stop along the way.

cards.title: How It Works
cards.body: Each input vector goes through linear layers that perform calculations, then through activation layers that determine which values to pass on. Finally, raw probability scores (logits) are created.

cards.title: Myth: Forward Pass Is Just Simple Math
cards.body: Truth: While it involves math, the forward pass is about transforming data through layers to make meaningful predictions.

cards.title: Visualizing the Forward Pass
cards.body: Imagine input vectors entering different layers in the model and generating logits.

left.text: Input data goes into the model.

exercises.prompt: What is produced at the end of the forward pass?
exercises.explain: After processing through layers, the forward pass outputs logits, which are raw scores predicting each class's probability.

exercises.prompt: The forward pass makes decisions about weights in the network.
exercises.explain: False. The forward pass is about processing inputs through layers to generate outputs, not about deciding on weights.

exercises.prompt: During the forward pass, input vectors route through ______ and activation layers.
exercises.sentence: During the forward pass, input vectors route through linear and activation layers.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'linear and activation layers' as they are crucial components for processing inputs in the forward pass.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes the forward pass?
exercises.explain: The forward pass processes inputs through the model's layers to yield output predictions.

exercises.prompt: In the context of a neural network, what role do logits play after the forward pass?

options.text: They provide the final training weights.
They are used to compare predictions against actual labels.
They are input vectors that need to be adjusted.
They indicate the model's current state.

exercises.explain: Logits are the final output of the forward pass and are essential for comparing predictions to actual labels, which in turn helps in guiding weight adjustments in training.
